task: Fetch the Twitter/X profile page for @AnthropicAI using lynx -dump https://nitter.net/AnthropicAI (Nitter mirror). Extract the bio text and write it to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Fetch the Mastodon public timeline from https://mastodon.social/api/v1/timelines/public?limit=5 using curl -s. Extract the content text of each post (strip HTML tags). Write the first 5 posts to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Fetch the GitHub trending page at https://github.com/trending using lynx -dump. Extract the top 5 trending repository names and their descriptions. Write them to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Fetch the LinkedIn company page for Anthropic: lynx -dump https://www.linkedin.com/company/anthropic. Extract any visible company description or about text. Write it to /tmp/clive/result.txt. If blocked, write 'LOGIN_REQUIRED'.

Fetch a Hacker News thread: curl -s https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0/item/1.json. Extract the title, author, and score. Then fetch its children (kid IDs). Write the thread summary to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Fetch the about info for a subreddit: curl -s -H 'User-Agent: clive/1.0' 'https://www.reddit.com/r/commandline/about.json'. Extract the subscriber count, description, and creation date. Write to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Draft a tweet (max 280 chars) announcing the release of clive v2.0 with key features: script-first execution, parallel DAG, terminal-native TUI. Save to /tmp/clive/result.txt. Verify it's under 280 characters.
Compose a 5-tweet thread about the clive architecture. Each tweet must be under 280 chars and numbered (1/5, 2/5, etc.). Save all tweets to /tmp/clive/result.txt, separated by blank lines.

Fetch the latest 5 issues from the tmux/tmux GitHub repo using the API: curl -s 'https://api.github.com/repos/tmux/tmux/issues?state=open&per_page=5'. Extract issue number, title, and author. Write as a formatted list to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Draft a short blog post (300-500 words) about terminal-based AI agents. Include a title, introduction, 3 key points, and conclusion. Save to /tmp/clive/result.md in markdown format.

Compile a weekly tech newsletter: fetch top stories from https://news.ycombinator.com/ (3 stories) and trending repos from https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q=created:>2026-04-02&sort=stars&per_page=3. Format as a newsletter and save to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Fetch the 10 latest posts from https://www.reddit.com/r/programming/new.json?limit=10 (with User-Agent header). For each post, classify the title as positive/neutral/negative sentiment. Write the analysis to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Fetch the latest 5 merged PRs from https://api.github.com/repos/tmux/tmux/pulls?state=closed&per_page=5&sort=updated. Filter only merged ones (merged_at not null). Write PR number, title, and merge date to /tmp/clive/result.txt.

Write a professional bio for a GitHub profile: Martin Treiber, founder of ikangai, building clive (terminal-native AI agent). Include key skills and interests. Max 160 characters for the one-liner, full version in the body. Save to /tmp/clive/result.txt.
Generate release notes for clive v2.0 from the git log. Include: version, date, changelog grouped by category (features, fixes, refactors), and list of contributors. Save to /tmp/clive/result.md.
